event: 3 weeks of lower extremity edema, fatigue, and shortness of breath
admitted to the medical intensive care unit (ICU) for resuscitation and urgent endoscopy
endoscopy, which revealed multiple gastric ulcers
pathology findings consistent with Hodgkin lymphoma
Acute renal failure requiring temporary hemodialysis
septic shock requiring vasopressor support with norepinephrine at a dose of 18 μg/min
telemetry displayed a sudden onset of ST-segment elevations
on placement of the ultrasound probe in the epigastric region, there was abrupt resolution of ST-segment elevations on telemetry
gentle palpation of the abdomen led to immediate resolution of ST-segment elevations
gastric decompression with nasogastric tube placement
Magnetic resonance imaging of the brain confirmed leptomeningeal enhancement concerning for metastatic spread
patient was placed on comfort care because of his grave prognosis
Following release of abdominal pressure, ST-segment elevations were demonstrated again
ECG following gastrointestinal decompression demonstrating normal sinus rhythm with resolution of ST-segment elevations
Further work-up revealed diffuse lymphadenopathy concerning for metastatic lymphoma
Arterial blood gas showed a pH of 7.40
Abdominal radiograph demonstrated significant gastric distention
Physical examination was notable for significant abdominal distention
no murmurs were detected on cardiac auscultation
oxygen saturation of 99% on mechanical ventilation
apparent ST-segment elevations in the precordial leads
J-point depression in the inferior leads
ECG was interpreted by the computer algorithm as ST-segment elevation myocardial infarction (STEMI)
Bedside transthoracic echocardiogram showed left ventricular ejection fraction to be 60% to 65%
no significant degree of abdominal compression associated with the ultrasound examination
no further documented ST-segment changes on telemetry or repeat ECG
did not demonstrate bradycardia or fluctuations in heart rate
patient was sedated with continuous infusion of propofol
patient was sedated with continuous infusion of fentanyl
